matchDay.title: Match Day
matchDay.transitStep1: Take the Trinity Railway Express (TRE) from Victory Station in downtown Dallas or Fort Worth Central Station.
matchDay.transitStep2: Exit at CentrePort/DFW Airport Station. Match ticket holders receive complimentary charter buses to the stadium bus hub.
matchDay.transitStep3: It's a 10-minute walk from the bus hub to Dallas Stadium.
matchDay.transitStep4: Download the GoPass app to purchase TRE tickets and get transit updates.
matchDay.parkingTip: Arrive early — stadium lots fill quickly on match days.
matchDay.layersActive: Showing parking, food & bars near stadium

gettingAround.title: Getting Around
gettingAround.description: Transit, trains, and rideshare in DFW
gettingAround.dartBody: Dallas Area Rapid Transit (DART) operates light rail across Dallas and suburbs. The Green and Orange lines serve Victory Station — your gateway to match-day TRE service.
gettingAround.treBody: TRE connects downtown Dallas and Fort Worth. On match days, take TRE to CentrePort/DFW Airport Station for free charter buses to the stadium (ticket holders).
gettingAround.gopassBody: Download GoPass to buy transit tickets on your phone. It covers DART, TRE, and other regional transit agencies.
gettingAround.rideshareBody: Uber and Lyft operate throughout DFW. At the stadium, expect surge pricing after matches — consider TRE or pre-book parking.

knowBefore.title: Know Before You Go
knowBefore.emergencyBody: Dial 911 for police, fire, or medical emergencies. This works from any phone, even without a local SIM.
knowBefore.tippingBody: Tips are expected in the US: 18–20% at restaurants, $1–2 per drink at bars, and a few dollars for rideshare/taxi.
knowBefore.weatherBody: Expect hot Texas summers: 90–100°F (32–38°C). Stay hydrated, wear sunscreen, and plan indoor breaks during midday.
knowBefore.powerBody: US outlets are Type A/B (120V). Pick up a prepaid SIM at the airport or use eSIM. Free Wi-Fi is common at hotels and cafés.

bbq.title: Texas BBQ Trail
bbq.introBody: North Texas is legendary for brisket, ribs, and sausage smoked low and slow over oak and pecan wood. From classic joints to modern pitmasters, you'll find BBQ within minutes of downtown.
bbq.tipsBody: Popular spots sell out by early afternoon. Go before noon on weekends. Many places are counter-service — grab a tray, find a seat, and enjoy.

deepEllum.title: Deep Ellum
deepEllum.introBody: Deep Ellum is Dallas's historic entertainment district — murals, live music venues, craft cocktail bars, and late-night eats. A perfect pre- or post-match hangout.

stockyards.title: Fort Worth Stockyards
stockyards.introBody: The Fort Worth Stockyards offer cattle drives, honky-tonks, BBQ, and boot shops. Experience authentic Texas culture just west of Dallas.
